---
pagetitle: "Simon Hamra · Labor market data with PySpark"
description: "Business analytics student at Boston University. I turn large Lightcast job posting data into clear answers about pay, skills and demand, with PySpark and machine learning."
page-layout: custom
include-in-header:
  - text: |
      <meta property="og:title" content="Simon Hamra · Labor market data with PySpark">
      <meta property="og:description" content="I turn large job posting data into clear answers about pay, skills and demand.">
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.hero__glow}
:::
::: {.wrap}
<span class="eyebrow reveal">Boston University · AD688 Applied Business Analytics</span>

# [Simon Hamra.]{.reveal .d1}<br>[A course portfolio]{.reveal .d2} [in labor data.]{.accent .reveal .d2}

::: {.hero__thesis .reveal .d3}
This site collects the three projects I built for my graduate analytics course at Boston University. All three run on the same large Lightcast job posting data: I clean and chart a 700 MB file in PySpark, model it as a small warehouse in Spark SQL, and train machine learning models to predict pay. It is coursework, but the numbers are real and I kept the hard parts in.
:::

::: {.btn-row .reveal .d4}
[See my projects](projects.qmd){.btn .btn--brand}
[Download CV](assets/cv/Simon-Hamra-CV.pdf){.btn .btn--ghost}
[Email me](mailto:simhamra@gmail.com){.btn .btn--link}
:::

::: {.hero__viz .reveal .d5}
![](assets/charts/pay_by_occupation.png)
<figcaption>From the visualization project (Assignment 3): median advertised salary by occupation across the Lightcast postings, using real observed pay only. The engineering-heavy roles sit at the top.</figcaption>
:::
:::
:::

::: {.section .section--tight}
::: {.wrap}
::: {.chips}
::: {.chip}
**700 MB** <span>read and cleaned in Spark</span>
:::
::: {.chip}
**72,498** <span>job postings analyzed</span>
:::
::: {.chip}
**3 models** <span>compared on real salaries</span>
:::
::: {.chip}
**57%** <span>of pay driven by experience</span>
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.section}
::: {.wrap}
::: {.section__head}
<span class="eyebrow">Course work</span>

## Three assignments, one dataset, real numbers
These are the three graded projects from my AD688 analytics course, each on the same Lightcast job postings data. Every one shows a different skill, and I kept the heavy work in Spark and only pulled small tables out to draw the charts.
:::

::: {.bento}

<div class="pcard col-4">
<a class="pcard__link" href="projects.qmd#a03">Salary insights at scale</a>
<div class="pcard__media"><img src="assets/charts/pay_by_education.png" alt="Median salary by education level"></div>
<div class="pcard__body">
<span class="pcard__meta">Assignment 3 · PySpark · Visualization</span>
<h3>Salary insights at scale</h3>
<p>I loaded a 700 MB file in Spark, cleaned the messy salary and education fields, filled the gaps in a careful way, and built charts that explain pay across industry, education and remote work.</p>
<div class="tags"><span class="tag">PySpark</span><span class="tag">pandas</span><span class="tag">Plotly</span><span class="tag">Seaborn</span></div>
<div class="pcard__foot"><span class="pcard__result">72,498 postings</span><span class="pcard__go">Read case study →</span></div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="pcard col-2">
<a class="pcard__link" href="projects.qmd#a05">Predicting salary, and a trap</a>
<div class="pcard__media"><img src="assets/charts/salary_drivers.png" alt="What moves salary, feature importance"></div>
<div class="pcard__body">
<span class="pcard__meta">Assignment 5 · Machine learning</span>
<h3>Predicting salary, and a trap</h3>
<p>Linear, polynomial and random forest models. Spark refused to give me standard errors, so I had to find out why.</p>
<div class="tags"><span class="tag">Spark ML</span><span class="tag">Random forest</span></div>
<div class="pcard__foot"><span class="pcard__result">+$8k / year of experience</span><span class="pcard__go">→</span></div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="pcard pcard--wide col-6">
<a class="pcard__link" href="projects.qmd#a02">A job market in SQL</a>
<div class="pcard__media"><img src="assets/charts/pay_by_state.png" alt="Top paying states"></div>
<div class="pcard__body">
<span class="pcard__meta">Assignment 2 · Spark SQL · Data modeling</span>
<h3>A job market in SQL</h3>
<p>I broke one flat file into clean relational tables for companies, industries and locations, then answered four real questions about pay, remote work, and how hiring moves over the year.</p>
<div class="tags"><span class="tag">Spark SQL</span><span class="tag">Star schema</span><span class="tag">Joins</span><span class="tag">matplotlib</span></div>
<div class="pcard__foot"><span class="pcard__result">1 star schema, 4 queries</span><span class="pcard__go">Read case study →</span></div>
</div>
</div>

:::
:::
:::

::: {.section .section--tight}
::: {.wrap}
::: {.bento}
::: {.card .col-4}
<span class="eyebrow">A short word</span>

### From marketing to machine learning
I started in business and marketing in France, then did two internships in e-commerce and social media. Along the way I found that I like the data part the most. Now I study applied business analytics at Boston University and I am teaching myself machine learning on top of the course.

[More about me](about.qmd){.btn--link}
:::
::: {.card .col-2}
<span class="eyebrow">Get in touch</span>

### Open to ML roles
I am looking for a real first experience in machine learning or data. The fastest way to reach me is email.

[simhamra@gmail.com](mailto:simhamra@gmail.com){.btn--link}
:::
:::
:::
:::
